seoTitle: How to Blur Out Parts of a Video in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to blur out portions of a video in Canva to hide sensitive details or draw focus, using overlay shapes and effects.
intro: Blurring part of a video is useful for protecting private information, hiding distracting background details, or directing the viewer's focus to what matters most. Canva doesn't have a one-click blur tool for video regions, but you can achieve the effect using overlay elements. This guide walks through the process.

steps.heading: Add your video to the canvas
steps.body: Upload your video clip and place it on your design as the base layer.

steps.heading: Create a blur overlay shape
steps.body: Add a shape or frosted-glass style element over the area you want to obscure, matching it to the size of that section.

steps.heading: Adjust the overlay's transparency and blend
steps.body: Set the overlay's opacity so it visually obscures the area without looking like a harsh block.

steps.heading: Position it to follow the footage
steps.body: If the video moves, adjust the overlay's position or use keyframes/animation so it stays over the correct spot.

steps.heading: Preview and export
steps.body: Play back the clip to confirm the blurred area stays covered, then export your finished video.

faq.question: Does Canva have a dedicated video blur tool?
faq.answer: Not a single built-in blur effect for specific regions — it's achieved by layering shapes or overlays over the area.

faq.question: Will the blur move with the footage automatically?
faq.answer: Not automatically; you may need to manually adjust the overlay's position at different points if the subject moves.

faq.question: Can I blur the entire video instead of just part of it?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva does offer a whole-clip blur effect that's separate from covering just one region.

faq.question: Is this technique available on the free plan?
faq.answer: Yes, the shapes and transparency tools needed are available without Canva Pro.
